message.content: A deductible is the amount you pay out-of-pocket for healthcare services before your insurance begins to cover costs. For your Blue Cross Blue Shield PPO Gold plan, you have a deductible of $2000 for individual coverage and $4000 for family coverage. Since you've already paid $1340 towards your individual deductible, you need to cover an additional $660 before your insurance starts to share the costs.

structuredResponse.recommendation: Understand that you will need to pay your remaining deductible amount before your insurance starts to cover most costs.
structuredResponse.coverageEstimate: Once the deductible is met, your plan will cover 80% of in-network costs, and you will be responsible for the remaining 20%.
structuredResponse.financialImpact: Paying the remaining deductible will contribute to your out-of-pocket maximum. After reaching the deductible, your coinsurance will apply to further costs.
assumptions: The remaining deductible will be paid in full before incurring additional medical costs.
Costs incurred are for in-network services to take advantage of the higher coinsurance.
No additional medical services will push you over the out-of-pocket maximum this year.
followUpQuestions: Do you have upcoming medical expenses that you are concerned about?
Would you like to know more about how coinsurance works?
Are you considering any specific healthcare services soon?
